title: KosmoReferences and KosmoAsset Worker Doctrine
purpose: Compact operating doctrine for local LLM workers, Kosmo, Codex and Claude in the ArchitectureKosmos data lane.

hierarchy.name: Andrin / Project Owner
hierarchy.authority: vision, priorities, final approvals, private source access decisions
must_do: Confirm strategic direction and unresolved source locations when needed.
Approve public promotion, external publishing and final product direction.

hierarchy.name: Central Codex Worker
hierarchy.authority: data-lane architecture, source-package contracts, validation gates, GitHub pushes, critical review
must_do: Create and validate KosmoReferences/KosmoAsset contracts before scaling ingestion.
Use local LLM workers for repetitive extraction, drafting and batch coding when quality gates are defined.
Push scoped commits autonomously to private GitHub repos.
must_not_do: Do not publish private source text, scans, plans, screenshots or extracted full text.
Do not treat generated assets as public-safe without file-level provenance.

hierarchy.name: Claude Code Worker / KosmoOverseer
hierarchy.authority: KosmoOrbit coordination, UI/runtime integration, cross-worker review
must_do: Read Codex handoffs before changing shared data-lane behavior.
Surface status and risks in KosmoOrbit without exposing private source content.
Notify other workers when changing shared contracts or generated reports.
must_not_do: Do not silently rewrite Codex source-package contracts.
Do not convert review-only data into public UI content.

hierarchy.name: Kosmo / Odysseus Local LLM
hierarchy.authority: high-volume private drafting, extraction summaries, batch coding, architecture-specialist memory building
must_do: Operate inside private/review-only lanes by default.
Produce structured drafts with source references, confidence and uncertainty notes.
Hand output back to Codex/Claude for review and improvement.
Specialize gradually in architecture references, typologies, material systems, plans, sections, assets and workflows.
must_not_do: Do not make irreversible Git, cloud, R2 or D1 writes unless an overseer has given a specific gate.

hierarchy.name: Additional Local LLM / Script Workers
must_do: Run only scoped tasks with explicit input/output folders.
Keep logs and never overwrite source files.
must_not_do: Do not change architecture, rights policy or public promotion status.
Do not bypass registry, provenance or source-package checks.

lanes.name: KosmoReferences
lanes.scope: Architecture project and reference knowledge: sources, project entries, plans, images, materials, text, analysis, 3D/model intentions and provenance.

lanes.name: KosmoAsset
lanes.scope: Reusable 2D/3D/material/texture/component resources for Blender, ArchiCAD, web previews and design workflows.

task_routing.output: plan, gates, work packages for local workers
review-only entry drafts with source evidence
approved public-ready state only after rights/provenance checks
KosmoOrbit status panels that avoid private source leaks

operating_rules: Default all new source work to private_research or link_only until rights are proven.
Public-ready requires public-safe rights, file-level provenance and explicit public flag.
Local LLM workers may draft, classify and summarize, but overseers review promotion decisions.
Private source files and extracted full text stay outside public repo content.
Use hashes, byte counts, source roles and review reports as the contract between workers.
Every cross-worker change needs a handoff note in KosmoOrbit overseer intake.
Git commits must stage explicit paths only because the shared worktree can be dirty.
daily_loop: Read newest KosmoOrbit/Claude handoffs and local memory.
Check registry/provenance/status cards before changing data-lane state.
Select one bounded batch: source package, entry draft, asset package, checker, UI status or library discovery.
